paper_title: Field and Laboratory Measurements of Deformation Bands in Sandstone

methods.description: Fieldwork conducted to measure lithological, structural, gamma-ray, and permeability characteristics of sandstone formations to understand deformation band distribution.

steps.description: Sections were measured with 30 cm (1 ft) increment, where for each point lithological description and measurements of gamma-ray, permeability, and structural elements were made.
steps.searchable_summary: measure lithological and structural elements at 30 cm intervals

steps.description: A detailed description of facies was made both across the stratigraphy and laterally along individual sandstone units.
steps.searchable_summary: describe facies across stratigraphy and laterally

steps.description: Deformation bands were counted using the scanline approach along 1 m sections perpendicular to the measurements path.
steps.searchable_summary: count deformation bands using scanline approach

steps.description: Orientations (strike and dip) of individual layers were measured using a Brunton compass.
steps.searchable_summary: measure strike and dip of layers

steps.description: Shale volume (V shale) was calculated from outcrop GR logs using Steiber's 1970 empirical equation.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate shale volume using Steiber's equation

steps.description: Sandstone net-to-gross (NTG) was calculated using the 'sandwich net to gross' approach.
Permeability measurements were taken using a portable permeameter (Tiny Perm II).
steps.searchable_summary: measure permeability with Tiny Perm II

methods.description: Laboratory analysis of rock samples to evaluate rock properties at the pore scale using petrography, RCAL, SEM, XRD, and CT.

steps.description: Sister-plugs with and without a deformation band were sampled within a short distance from each other.
Minerals were identified via XRD and SEM measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: identify minerals using XRD and SEM

steps.description: Porosity and permeability of the plugs were measured using helium porosimeter and air permeameter.
DB permeability was derived using a simple harmonic averaging methodology of serial beds.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate DB permeability using harmonic averaging

methods.method_name: Sandstone Composition and V Shale Approach
methods.description: Analysis of sandstone composition and application of V shale approach to predict deformation bands.

steps.description: V shale cutoff values were applied to quantify formation conditions of deformation bands.
Calcite content was analyzed to understand its effect on DB concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze calcite content effect on DB

methods.description: Field-scale investigation of the effect of net-to-gross succession on deformation band occurrence.

steps.description: Investigate if intermediate to low net-to-gross succession affects deformation band occurrence.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze net-to-gross effect on DB occurrence
